    "R1.1",

    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",

    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should I prioritize: (A) surface-level polish (grammar, clarity, concision) without changing structure, claims, or adding content, or (B) substantive revision including reorganization, clearer contribution framing, and tightening claim–evidence alignment and novelty positioning (may add or move sentences)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improving the draft may require reframing the contribution and clarifying the thesis of RetrieveCascade beyond sentence-level edits.",
    "R1.2": "The claim of competitive performance under differing protocols should be presented with a coherent premise-to-conclusion chain.",
    "R1.5": "The method, dataset, and results sections likely benefit from reordering and a clearer narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "The prose needs grammar, clarity, and concision fixes to read professionally and unambiguously.",
    "RX.1": "Any improvement should flag uncertainties around indirect comparisons and evaluation protocol differences.",
    "RX.2": "The rewrite must avoid overstating performance given indirect baselines and resource constraints.",
    "RX.3": "Edits should stay within the chosen improvement scope without adding out-of-scope content.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to avoid misrepresenting findings that could mislead readers about retrieval performance.",
    "RX.5": "Revisions should preserve traceability to the original text and label any added assumptions or rephrasings."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should I prioritize: (A) surface-level polish (grammar, clarity, concision) without changing structure, claims, or adding content, or (B) substantive revision including reorganization, clearer contribution framing, and tightening claim–evidence alignment and novelty positioning (may add or move sentences)?

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft may require reframing the contribution and clarifying the thesis of RetrieveCascade beyond sentence-level edits.
rationale.R1.2: The claim of competitive performance under differing protocols should be presented with a coherent premise-to-conclusion chain.
rationale.R1.5: The method, dataset, and results sections likely benefit from reordering and a clearer narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: The prose needs grammar, clarity, and concision fixes to read professionally and unambiguously.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should flag uncertainties around indirect comparisons and evaluation protocol differences.
rationale.RX.2: The rewrite must avoid overstating performance given indirect baselines and resource constraints.
rationale.RX.3: Edits should stay within the chosen improvement scope without adding out-of-scope content.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid misrepresenting findings that could mislead readers about retrieval performance.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions should preserve traceability to the original text and label any added assumptions or rephrasings.
